name: Bitbucket
description: Atlassian's cloud Git hosting and Pipelines CI/CD platform; yields workspace audit log, permission audit and branch restriction evidence.

steps.action: Navigate to bitbucket.org > your workspace > Workspace settings, then under Security select Audit log
steps.audit_note: The workspace audit log retains roughly the last 30 days of events, so a weekly capture cadence avoids gaps

steps.action: Filter for key events (user added/removed from workspace, repository created/deleted, settings changed) and take dated screenshots — the workspace audit log has no CSV export; historic data can be requested from Atlassian Support if required
steps.audit_note: Include the browser date/time and workspace name in each screenshot to preserve evidentiary context

steps.action: For the workspace permission audit, navigate to Workspace settings > User groups and screenshot each group's permission level and membership

For branch protection evidence, open each critical repository, select Repository settings, then under Workflow select Branch restrictions, and screenshot the configured restrictions (write access, merge access, merge checks)
steps.audit_note: Capture the current configuration directly — branch restriction changes may not appear in the workspace audit log, so the screenshot is the primary record

steps.action: Upload the screenshots to the evidence repository with the evidence ID reference
steps.security_note: Ensure no API tokens, app passwords or pipeline variable values are visible in screenshots

L2.title: Atlassian Guard Organisation Audit Log Exports
L2.estimated_time: 30 minutes initial setup, then 10 minutes per export

steps.action: Link the Bitbucket workspace to your Atlassian organisation and confirm an Atlassian Guard subscription — this enables the organisation-level audit log for Bitbucket events
Navigate to admin.atlassian.com > Security > Audit log, filter by product (Bitbucket) and set the date range for the reporting period
Select Export to download a CSV of the filtered events (up to the 10,000 most recent events per export)
steps.audit_note: Guard retains audit log activity for up to 180 days; exporting monthly preserves evidence before the retention window closes

steps.action: Bitbucket has no natively scheduled evidence delivery, so set a recurring monthly task for the export and record each run against the evidence ID
steps.audit_note: Note that Bitbucket events are being added to the Guard audit log incrementally; verify the event types you rely on actually appear

steps.action: File each CSV in the evidence repository with restricted access
steps.security_note: Exports contain user email addresses and IP addresses; store them in a location with access limited to the compliance team

steps.action: Create credentials: on a Premium plan, create a workspace access token (restricted to the single workspace, with read-only scopes and an expiry); otherwise create a scoped API token at id.atlassian.com > Security > API tokens — API tokens are the long-term replacement for the deprecated app passwords
steps.permissions_required: Workspace Admin (workspace access token) or individual Atlassian account (API token)
steps.security_note: Prefer a workspace access token for service automation as it is not tied to a person; store either token in a secrets manager — they cannot be viewed again after creation

steps.action: Authenticate API calls with Basic auth (Atlassian account email + API token) or a Bearer header for access tokens, per the Bitbucket Cloud REST API authentication guidance
steps.security_note: Send credentials only over HTTPS and never log the Authorization header

steps.action: Run a daily workspace permission audit: GET https://api.bitbucket.org/2.0/workspaces/{workspace}/permissions for workspace members and their permission (owner/collaborator/member), and GET .../permissions/repositories for effective repository permissions
steps.permissions_required: Workspace Admin (the authenticating identity must be a workspace admin)
steps.audit_note: The repository permissions endpoint returns effective permissions, so group-derived access is included — this is what an access review needs

steps.action: Export branch protection configuration per repository: GET https://api.bitbucket.org/2.0/repositories/{workspace}/{repo_slug}/branch-restrictions for every in-scope repository
steps.permissions_required: Repository Admin on the target repositories

steps.action: Collect organisation audit events programmatically: create an organisation API key in admin.atlassian.com, then call GET https://api.atlassian.com/admin/v1/orgs/{orgId}/events with Bearer authentication and from/to/cursor parameters
steps.permissions_required: Organisation Admin (to create the API key)
steps.security_note: Organisation API keys are highly privileged; scope usage to read operations, store the key securely and rotate it regularly
steps.audit_note: This endpoint is rate-limited to around 10 requests per minute; batch daily pulls rather than polling aggressively

steps.action: Add error handling and validation: follow pagination 'next' links to completion, alert on authentication failures or empty result windows, and store outputs with run timestamps and checksums
steps.audit_note: Recording run metadata and checksums makes each collected file independently verifiable

steps.action: Bitbucket Cloud has no native SIEM audit streaming; the closest real capability is continuous cursor-based polling of the Atlassian organisation events API (GET https://api.atlassian.com/admin/v1/orgs/{orgId}/events, with the events-stream variant recommended for higher-frequency collection)

Supplement with repository webhooks: in each in-scope repository, go to Repository settings > Webhooks and register a collector endpoint for push, pull request and merge events; configure the webhook secret so the collector can validate request signatures
steps.security_note: Use an HTTPS endpoint and reject webhook deliveries that fail signature validation to keep forged events out of the evidence store

steps.action: Run a scheduled Bitbucket Pipelines job that verifies branch restrictions and workspace permissions against your approved baseline via the REST API, raising an alert on any drift
steps.permissions_required: Repository Admin (pipeline configuration); workspace access token for the API calls

steps.action: Reconcile daily: compare webhook-received event counts against the organisation events API for the same window, and alert on gaps or webhook delivery failures
steps.audit_note: Completeness metrics from reconciliation are what turn continuous collection into audit-grade evidence

steps.action: Publish a compliance dashboard: branch restriction compliance rate across repositories, workspace admin count trend and access review coverage, reported weekly
Quarterly, rotate API tokens, workspace access tokens and organisation API keys, and review the workspace admin and organisation admin lists
steps.security_note: Expired or orphaned automation credentials are a common finding; tie rotation to the quarterly access review
